package_manifest:
# AIL Package

name: support-ticket
version: 0.1.0
profile: Application
entry: spec.ail-spec.md
features: things, actions, failures, guarantees, traces, secrets
conformance: first-slice
target-support:
  linux-x86_64-elf: supported
  wasm32-unknown-sandbox-wasm: supported-with-host-imports
  aarch64-apple-darwin-libsystem-macho: planned-contract
requirements:
AIL-Requirements:
- The application Support Tickets manages customer support tickets, assignments, updates, internal notes, and overdue-ticket review.
- The application users are Customer, Support agent, and Support manager.
- A User has fields id: Text, role: State<Customer, SupportAgent, SupportManager>, and email: Text.
- A Ticket has fields id: Text, title: Text, status: State<New, Open, Assigned, Closed, Overdue>, customer: User, assignee: Option<User>, created_at: Time, due_at: Time, public_updates: List<Text>, and internal notes: Secret<List<Text>>.
- The application shows an open ticket queue for support agents.
- The application shows an Overdue tickets view for support managers.
- The application shows a customer-visible ticket history that includes public updates and never includes internal notes.
- The Create ticket action is performed by a customer.
- Create ticket requires the customer id and title.
- Create ticket creates a Ticket with status New, records the customer as the ticket customer, records an initial public update, guarantees internal notes are empty and secret, and records a trace event named TicketCreated.
- The Assign ticket action is performed by a support agent.
- Assign ticket requires the ticket to exist, requires the ticket status to be New or Open, and requires the assignee role to be SupportAgent or SupportManager.
- Assign ticket changes the ticket assignee, changes the status to Assigned, records a public update, guarantees the assignee can see internal notes, and records a trace event named TicketAssigned.
- The Close ticket action is performed by a support agent.
- Close ticket requires the ticket to exist and requires the ticket status not to be Closed.
- Close ticket changes the ticket status to Closed, records a public update, does not reveal internal notes to the customer, guarantees closed tickets do not appear in the open ticket queue, and records a trace event named TicketClosed.
- The scheduler marks overdue tickets by reading tickets whose status is New, Open, or Assigned.
- Marking overdue tickets requires the current time to be later than due_at, changes the ticket status to Overdue, records a public update, and records a trace event named TicketOverdue.
- Failure NotFound happens when a ticket id does not match a stored ticket, changes no ticket data, shows Ticket not found, and the trace records TicketNotFound.
- Failure PermissionDenied happens when a user tries to see internal notes without support staff permission, reveals no secret value, shows Permission denied, and the trace records InternalNotesDenied.
